gene_region.label: CRP transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical CRP genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window relative to the reverse-strand transcription start, used to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: CRP encodes C-reactive protein, a pentraxin acute-phase protein. This bundle emphasizes acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local CRP bundle is acute-phase inflammatory biomarker review oriented. Interpret markers through exact allele class, zygosity, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context rather than as stand-alone deterministic calls.
variant_effect_overview: CRP variants can alter or tag acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response; effect direction depends on allele class, tissue, dosage, and biological context.
Common markers, pharmacogenetic alleles, structural events, and high-impact pathogenic variants should be kept separate during interpretation.
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: CRP methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for CRP.
Because CRP biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use CRP promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.
Variant-plus-methylation review for acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000132693: CRP coordinate source

biorender_visuals.label: CRP biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: CRP: acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response
recommended_icons: Vessel cross-section with red  blood cells (anemic)
Blood vessel (with red  blood cells, anemic)
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a CRP acute-phase inflammatory biomarker review thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response and Inflammation biomarker context, Cardiometabolic association research. Treat this as pathway triage until the exact allele has external classification and phenotype support.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1205 (CRP 3'UTR marker) is curated as a CRP marker for Inflammation biomarker context, Cardiometabolic association research. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Curated research and clinical-context marker; external clinical classification is required for diagnostic use.
functional_effects: Can modify or tag acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
research_context: Use this marker as a CRP pathway-context signal, not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Sequence, methylation, expression, and protein data may point in different directions and should be reconciled.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: Pair rs1205 (CRP 3'UTR marker) with CRP methylation only as local regulatory context; methylation does not prove the variant's biochemical effect.

literature_findings.genotypes: rs1205 (CRP 3'UTR marker) or linked CRP locus genotype states
literature_findings.phenotype: Inflammation biomarker context, Cardiometabolic association research
literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs1205 (CRP 3'UTR marker) as a gene-specific marker for Inflammation biomarker context, Cardiometabolic association research and acute-phase inflammation, complement engagement, pentraxin biology, cardiometabolic biomarker context, and hepatic IL-6 response.
